methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Nano One Materials is a public company trading on the TSX in the Climate & Energy sector. The company has raised approximately C$100 million to date. The valuation landscape for this stage and sector is sparse: the comparable universe consists of only 1 public peer (LNZA) and 8 private peers, including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape. While 19 companies operate within Climate & Energy broadly, the narrow peer set severely constrains traditional valuation methods.

The peer-median approach is not applicable here. A search for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier peers within Climate & Energy yielded zero datapoints meeting minimum thresholds (need ≥3 for statistical reliability). Relying on an all-sector median would introduce material noise and obscure the specific risk and growth profile of early-stage deep-tech businesses in this domain.

Without sufficient narrow comparables, quantitative triangulation cannot be completed on the data provided. Resolution requires either: (1) expansion of the comp set to include earlier or later-stage peers with documented valuations; (2) application of alternative methods (DCF, cost-to-build, revenue multiple) contingent on financial projections; or (3) explicit weighting of the single public peer alongside selected private transaction data. The primary uncertainty remains the lack of homogeneous valuation precedents.
